Title: Wheat yellow mosaic virus NIb targets TaVTC2 to elicit broad-spectrum pathogen resistance in wheat

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Respiratory burst oxidase homolog; NADPH oxidase family
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Silenced by BSMV-VIGS in wheat seedlings
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immune signaling and antiviral defense
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ROS accumulation and inhibits WYMV infection; silencing reduces ROS and increases WYMV accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaRBOHD, a wheat homolog of Arabidopsis RBOHD, was identified as a ROS-production gene. 2. Silencing TaRBOHD strongly reduced ROS accumulation in wheat leaves. 3. TaRBOHD silencing significantly increased WYMV CP accumulation, showing that ROS accumulation restricts WYMV infection.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homolog identification; BSMV-VIGS silencing; qRT-PCR for silencing and WYMV CP; DAB and NBT staining
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing ROS production pathways such as TaRBOHD may support antiviral resistance, though no direct breeding test was performed
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed mainly as a supporting ROS-production factor rather than the primary central gene of the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; maize Ubi promoter for RNAi hairpin
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: L-galactose pathway of ascorbic acid (AsA) biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core AsA biosynthetic pathway, GDP-L-galactose conversion step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed mostly in leaves of wheat seedlings; induced by WYMV infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in leaves; expression increases with increasing WYMV accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of GDP-L-galactose and phosphate to L-galactose-1-phosphate and GDP
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed/rate-limiting midstream step in AsA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established rate-limiting enzyme of the AsA synthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies direct biosynthetic intermediate required for AsA production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; scavenges reactive oxygen species; modulates pathogen resistance
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: WYMV infection; exogenous AsA; exogenous H2O2; Pseudomonas syringae DC3000 challenge in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes AsA biosynthesis and susceptibility to WYMV; knockdown triggers ROS-mediated broad-spectrum resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaVTC2 was cloned from wheat and identified as a GDP-L-galactose phosphorylase involved in AsA biosynthesis. 2. TaVTC2 overexpression increased AsA content, reduced ROS accumulation, and enhanced WYMV accumulation and disease symptoms. 3. TaVTC2 knockdown/RNAi reduced AsA accumulation, caused ROS burst, suppressed WYMV infection, and conferred resistance to CWMV, BSMV, and Bgt. 4. WYMV NIb directly bound TaVTC2 and inhibited its enzymatic activity by reducing substrate affinity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; RNAi knockdown; BSMV-VIGS; metabolite quantification of AsA; enzyme assay; enzyme kinetics; HPLC; MST; ITC; Y2H; co-IP; GST pull-down; ROS staining; virus accumulation assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partial suppression of TaVTC2 is a potential strategy to enhance broad-spectrum disease resistance in wheat, but yield/seed weight penalties and reduced AsA should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: TaVTC2-RNAi lines showed slightly decreased seed number per ear and 1000-kernel weight; TaVTC2-OE plants showed inferior spike length and seed quality in virus-contaminated field
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors failed to obtain TaVTC2 knockout mutants. NIb inhibits TaVTC2 activity in a near 1:1 stoichiometric manner and weakens GDP-L-galactose binding. NIb interaction domain mapped to NIb amino acids 284-423.
